name: subjective_social_mobility
description: Captures respondents' perceived trajectory of economic and social standing across generations — backward-looking (own situation vs. parents), forward-looking (children vs. self), and structural beliefs about whether upward mobility is realistically achievable for young Mexicans today.

name: material_living_standards
description: Measures objective household material conditions through dwelling quality and asset ownership, forming a composite index of socioeconomic deprivation or well-being consistent with multidimensional poverty measurement frameworks.

name: structural_attribution_of_poverty
description: Assesses the degree to which respondents attribute poverty and social problems to systemic, institutional, or structural forces (e.g., government, elites, discrimination) rather than individual failings, reflecting ideological orientations toward inequality.

name: social_policy_preferences
description: Captures respondents' normative preferences regarding how anti-poverty policy should be designed, funded, prioritized, and delivered, including beliefs about government effectiveness and the appropriate role of different societal actors.

name: subjective_economic_wellbeing
description: Measures respondents' self-assessed economic and psychological well-being, including perceived personal control over economic outcomes, happiness, satisfaction with work and leisure, self-identified social class, and perceived resilience — capturing the subjective experience of living standards beyond objective material conditions.
